title: 10 best herbal essential oils for itchy eyes
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for itchy eyes are a natural solution to alleviate discomfort and irritation caused by dryness, allergies, or other conditions. These oils are extracted from plants and have anti-inflammatory, antihistamine, and moisturizing properties that help soothe and calm itchy eyes. Examples of herbal essential oils that help with itchy eyes include chamomile, calendula, and peppermint oil. By applying a few drops of these oils to the affected area or inhaling their vapors, individuals can experience relief from itchiness and redness, improving their overall quality of life and reducing eye strain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil contains calendic acid, which soothes and calms irritated skin and eyes, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, the antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, while also promoting healthy tear production. By applying pot marigold essential oils to the affected area, you can experience immediate relief from itchy eyes and promote long-term eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot Marigold essential oil reduces inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort caused by irritation or allergies.
Antibacterial: The oil's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause itchy eyes, such as conjunctivitis, by preventing the growth of bacteria and promoting healing.

Anti-irritant: Pot Marigold essential oil has a soothing effect on irritated skin and mucous membranes in the eyes, calming itchiness, redness, and discomfort caused by exposure to allergens or irritants.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing itchy eyes and reducing redness.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain a compound called calendulic acid, which has antihistamine properties that help alleviate itching and discomfort in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers and wash them gently in cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 50g of washed calendula flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14-21 days to allow for infusion and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Transfer the extracted herbal oil to small glass bottles and store them in a cool dry place for up to 6 months or use within 30 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with itchy eyes because of its soothing and calming properties. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness and swelling, while its antimicrobial agents help eliminate any irritants or allergens that may be causing the itchiness. Additionally, aloe's natural moisturizing abilities help to hydrate and comfort dry, irritated eyes, providing long-lasting relief from itchy, scratchy sensations.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Aloe essential oil's antibacterial property helps to reduce inflammation and eliminate bacterial infections that can cause itchiness and redness in the eyes.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory property of aloe essential oil soothes and calms irritated eye tissues, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with itchy eyes.
Antioxidant: Aloe essential oil's antioxidant property helps to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, further contributing to itchiness and dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the aloe plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming itchy eyes.
Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain natural anti-irritants and anti-inflammatories that help reduce redness and discomfort associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from your garden or local store ensuring they are free of pesticides.
Wash the harvested aloe vera leaves thoroughly with distilled water and mild soap solution.
Slice the washed aloe vera leaves into thin pieces and blend them with carrier oil coconut or sweet almond oil at 10 ratio.
Steep the blended mixture for 2 hours in a double boiler at low heat then filter it through cheesecloth or coffee filters.
Store the resulting herbal aloe vera essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight lids at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower essential oils helps with itchy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling. The oil's natural soothing qualities also calm irritated tissues and relieve discomfort, providing fast and effective relief from dry, itchy, or irritated eyes. Additionally, the antihistamine-like properties of coneflower essential oil help to neutralize histamine release, further reducing itching and inflammation for a more comfortable vision experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce redness and swelling in itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant: Echinacea's antioxidant properties can help protect the eyes against oxidative stress and damage caused by environmental factors, which may contribute to itchiness.
Immunomodulatory: Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects may help regulate the body's natural response to allergens or irritants that can cause itchy eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of Kansas coneflower are used to create essential oils that soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: The seeds contain valuable fatty acids and other nutrients that help reduce inflammation and itchiness associated with dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh echinacea angustifolia roots and clean them thoroughly.
Steam distill the cleaned roots in 100 milliliters of water for 2 hours and 30 minutes.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Transfer the filtered essential oil into a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting cap and store at room temperature for 14 days.
Dilute the stored essential oil with 10 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with itchy eyes because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds work to reduce redness and irritation, providing instant relief from itchy sensations. Additionally, lavender's natural antihistamine effects help to alleviate itching caused by allergies or environmental factors. By applying a few drops of the oil to the affected area, users can experience rapid comfort and relaxation, making English lavender essential oils a popular natural remedy for itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: English lavender essential oil has natural antihistamine properties that help to reduce itching and inflammation caused by allergies or irritants, providing relief for itchy eyes.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of lavender essential oil can help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, making them feel more comfortable and reducing itchiness.

Antioxidant properties: English lavender essential oil is rich in antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to itchy eyes and other eye issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itchy eyes.
Seeds: Lavender seeds are rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, which can help reduce redness and inflammation associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavender flowers of lavandula angustifolia in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers using a clean towel or paper bags for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Fill a clean glass jar one-third full with the dried lavender flowers and pour in 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Stir the mixture well and store it in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining the mixture through cheesecloth.
Transfer the filtered liquid to a clean glass bottle and label it; use within 6 months or discard if an off smell or texture develops.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with itchy eyes because of its soothing and calming properties. When applied topically, it can help to reduce inflammation and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds also work to calm the nerve endings around the eyes, reducing irritation and scratching sensations. Additionally, lemon balm essential oils have natural antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent infections that may contribute to itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort.
Antihistamine: The antihistamine properties in lemon balm essential oil can help alleviate itchiness by blocking the release of histamine, a chemical that causes allergic reactions and itching.
Antiviral: Lemon balm's antiviral properties may help combat viral infections such as conjunctivitis (pink eye), which can cause itchy eyes, by inhibiting the replication of viruses.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lemon balm leaves contain high amounts of citral and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain a higher concentration of essential oils than other parts of the plant, making them ideal for extracting medicinal properties to combat itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the early morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for 3 hours or air drying for 7 days.
Place 100 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves into a glass jar and cover with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 14 days allowing for slow infusion to occur.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container discarding solids and use immediately or store in airtight containers at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime essential oils helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The oil's antibacterial properties also help to reduce the risk of infections that can cause itchiness. Additionally, lime essential oil has a mild sedative effect, which can help to relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing tension and discomfort. When used in eye drops or added to a carrier oil for massage, herbal lime essential oils provide instant relief from itchy eyes, leaving them feeling calm and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal lime essential oil contains antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to itchiness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in herbal lime essential oil can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort associated with itchy eyes.

Antihistaminic properties: Herbal lime essential oil contains antihistamine compounds that can help block histamine receptors, which can alleviate itching and other allergy-related symptoms in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, making them ideal for reducing redness and itching.
Buds: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that provide potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, effective in alleviating eye discomfort and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality tilia europaea flowers at dawn on a warm day with low humidity.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days, stirring occasionally, to reduce moisture content.
Use a coffee grinder or spice mill to grind 50g of dried tilia europaea flowers into a fine powder, taking 5 minutes.
Combine the ground powder with 250ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil in a glass jar and leave it to infuse for 2 weeks, shaking daily.
Filter the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids, then store the resulting essential oil in an amber bottle, ready for use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort essential oils helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, the oil's antimicrobial effects help to combat any underlying infections that may be contributing to itchy eyes. As a result, St John's Wort essential oils provide fast and effective relief from itchiness and discomfort, promoting healthy and comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: St John's Wort essential oil has antioxidant properties that can help to neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the eyes, which may contribute to itchiness.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of St John's Wort essential oil can help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with itchy eyes.
Antiviral property: St John's Wort essential oil has antiviral properties that can help to combat viral infections such as conjunctivitis, which is a common cause of itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of St John's Wort contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that can help reduce redness and inflammation in itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st. john's wort flowers at dawn or dusk, taking care not to bruise the petals.
Dry the harvested st. john's wort flowers in a warm, dry place for 1-2 weeks or using desiccant.
Combine 100 grams of dried st. john's wort flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 hours under indirect sunlight and gentle heat, then strain through cheesecloth.
Transfer the infused oil to a dark glass bottle, allowing it to mature for 2-3 weeks before use as an eye drops solution.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry essential oils helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil contains potent antioxidants that reduce redness and swelling, providing quick relief from itchy and irritated eyes. Its astringent properties also help to constrict blood vessels, reducing the appearance of puffiness and dark circles under the eyes. Additionally, highbush cranberry essential oils have antimicrobial properties that combat infections and promote healthy tear production, further alleviating itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Highbush cranberry essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with irritated or inflamed eyes, providing relief from discomfort.

Antioxidant: As an antioxidant, highbush cranberry essential oil helps neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the eyes' delicate tissues, reducing the risk of oxidative stress and promoting healthy eye function.

Antimicrobial: Highbush cranberry essential oil's antimicrobial property fights off bacterial or fungal infections that may be contributing to itchy eyes, preventing further infection and promoting recovery from existing conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the highbush cranberry plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, making them ideal for reducing itchiness.
Fruits: The fruits of the highbush cranberry plant have antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from further irritation and inflammation, while also providing natural relief from itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality vaccinium macrocarpon leaves and berries in early morning or evening to avoid heat stress.
Dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator on low temperature setting for at least 12 hours or until crumbly.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a still with a 10:1 ratio of water to plant material at 100°c for 6-8 hours.
Filter the essential oil obtained from the distillation process using activated charcoal and filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the final vaccinium macrocarpon essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's antioxidants work to calm irritated skin and reduce redness, providing instant relief from itchy sensations. Additionally, chamomile's gentle, non-irritating nature makes it an excellent natural remedy for sensitive eyes. When applied topically or inhaled, the oil's calming effects can help to ease discomfort and promote relaxation, making it a natural and effective solution for soothing itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness associated with itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort and irritation.

Antihistamine: The antihistamine properties of chamomile essential oil help to neutralize histamines, which are the chemical mediators responsible for allergic reactions such as itching, redness, and tearing in the eyes.
Calming and soothing: Chamomile's calming and soothing properties promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can exacerbate itchy eyes caused by environmental triggers or other factors.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are a popular choice for making essential oils because they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that can help reduce eye irritation and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in their prime condition and dry them properly using air circulation or desiccant for 7-10 days.
Crush the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to release their aromatic oils.
Combine the powdered matricaria chamomilla with high-proof neutral alcohol in a ratio of 1:4 to create a maceration mixture that will infuse for 14 days.
Monitor the maceration mixture daily, gently shaking the container to ensure even extraction of essential oils over the course of two weeks before filtering it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Distill the filtered maceration mixture using steam distillation apparatus at atmospheric pressure for 2-3 hours to separate the essential oil from the plant material and water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date essential oils helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eye tissues. The oil's antioxidant capabilities also help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, Chinese date essential oil's gentle and non-irritating nature makes it an effective remedy for soothing red, itchy, and watery eyes, providing immediate relief from the annoyance of itchy eyes. Its benefits are especially pronounced when used in combination with other natural ingredients, such as tea tree oil and lavender oil.
remedy_properties: Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla): Chamomile essential oil has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm irritated eyes and reduce itchiness.

Ginkgo biloba: Ginkgo biloba is an herb that is often used in Traditional Chinese Medicine to treat eye disorders, including itchy eyes. Its essential oil may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and protect the eyes.

Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra): Licorice root has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various eye conditions, including dryness and itchiness. Its essential oil may have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with dry or irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ziziphus jujuba leaves and stems from the plant's mature branches.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area for at least 7 days.
Crush the dried plant material into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to release oils.
Steam distill the crushed plant material using 20g of plant material per 100ml of water for 2 hours.
Filter and store the resulting essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy eyes with herbal essential oils is by diluting a few drops of soothing chamomile oil or lavender oil in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Gently apply the mixture to the affected area using a clean cotton ball, taking care not to touch the eye itself. This will provide instant relief from itching and discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy eyes that are treated with herbal essential oils are skin irritations, such as eczema and acne. Essential oils like tea tree oil, lavender oil, and chamomile oil have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce redness and itching. They can also be used to treat other eye-related issues, including dry eyes and conjunctivitis, as well as minor cuts and burns on the skin.
